name: Sichuan Dry-Fried String Beans
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Preserved Sichuan Preserved, Vegetable (jar choy)
3 cups Peanut oil for deep frying
1 pound String beans, snapped in half
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, soak the dried shrimp in hot water for 30 minutes, then drain.
2. Chop the drained shrimp into coarse breadcrumb-sized pieces.
3. Rinse the Sichuan preserved vegetables with cold water and chop them to the same texture as the shrimp.
4. Heat peanut oil in a wok until it reaches 375°F (190°C).
5. Deep fry the string beans in two or three batches for 2-3 minutes, or until they are wrinkled, blistered, and have a khaki color.
6. Remove all but 1 tablespoon of oil from the wok and reheat over high heat.
7. Add ginger and garlic; stir-fry for 15 seconds.
8. Add pork, preserved mustard, shrimp; stir-fry for an additional minute.
9. Break up the clumps of pork so it looks crumbled.
10. Add green onion, sugar, white pepper, and dark soy sauce; toss to combine.
11. Return the reserved string beans, chicken stock, and a swirl of sesame oil to the wok.
12. Cook over high heat until all liquids are reduced and absorbed, about 2-3 minutes.
Serve hot or at room temperature. Yields 4 to 6 servings.
